summary: web3-secrets-detector exfiltrates host data and process environment to attacker-controlled C2
details: The package is a credential/system-information stealer disguised as a 'web3 secrets detector'. package.json line 30 contains an inline Node script that requires os/https/child_process, gathers host identifiers and process.env, and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js compounds the same behavior at scale: it imports fs/os/https/child_process (lines 4-9), enumerates filesystem state via fs.existsSync (lines 93, 114), runs execSync of system commands (e.g. whoami at line 233) and os.hostname() (lines 234, 246), and shells out via curl (lines 298, 323, 408). Collected data is shipped via https.request/https.get (lines 142-143, 161) to https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 161, 323) — a GitHub Pages user account being abused as a hardcoded C2 endpoint. The combined fingerprint (hardcoded C2 in a lifecycle inline script + system intel collection + child_process command harvesting + filesystem enumeration) is unambiguous installer-side data exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
